holdings.infoBox: EPP is an equity ETF with a total of 108 individual holdings. The top holdings are BHP Group stock at 10.43%, Commonwealth Bank of Australia at 8.53%, DBS Group Holdings at 4.55%, AIA Group at 4.49%, and Westpac Banking at 3.85%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI Pacific ex-Japan ETF (EPP) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI Pacific ex JP index, a market-cap-weighted index of companies in the Asia-Pacific region, excluding Japan. EPP was launched on Oct 25, 2001 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Why Japan's $70 billion-plus intervention and a rate hike didn't prop up the yen more
data.text: Japan spent about $73 billion on foreign exchange intervention and the Bank of Japan has raised rates, but the yen remains near 160 against the dollar. A wide U.S.-Japan rate gap, carry trades and Pri...

data.title: Japan's Consumer Inflation Held Steady in May
data.text: Consumer inflation remained steady last month, with economists noting that the full effect of higher oil prices has yet to emerge.

data.title: Bank of Japan Could Raise Rates Even If Mideast Uncertainty Persists
data.text: Japan's central bank could look past Middle East uncertainty and raise interest rates if inflation becomes a bigger threat to the economy than the risk of slowing growth, Gov. Kazuo Ueda said.

data.title: Japanese bond yields are the highest in 40 years. The budget and a 'red flag' from PM Takaichi have markets nervous

data.title: Foreign investors buy Japanese stocks for eighth week on AI rally
data.text: Foreign investors bought Japanese stocks for the eighth straight week through May 23, as oil prices eased and ​AI-related shares rallied on robust demand outlook.

data.title: Nikkei Rises 1.3%, Led by Chip, Metals Stocks
data.text: Japanese stocks were higher in early trade thanks to continued hopes for U.S.-Iran peace talks.

data.title: Japan Consumer Inflation Slows Despite Middle East Effects
data.text: Japan's consumer inflation cooled in April as government measures capped price growth despite rising energy costs.

data.title: Japan inflation softens more than expected in April, weakening case for BOJ rate hike
data.text: Energy costs remained in focus as lawmakers pushed subsidy measures. Strong exports supported growth and kept rate hike expectations alive.

data.title: Bank of Japan Policymaker Signals That Rate Hike Might Be Approaching
data.text: Policy board member Junko Koeda said Japan's underlying inflation is likely already at around 2%.

data.title: Japanese Stocks Are Bargains, Even After Big Gains
data.text: Value investors think the good times are far from over for the Japanese stock market. Here are stocks and funds to invest in now.

data.title: Goldman Sachs says AI and energy resilience are creating a North-South divide in Asian markets
data.text: Investors are focusing on AI developments in North Asia. China is coming out of more than three years of deflation measured by the producer price index.

data.title: Hong Kong gives initial approval to first bitcoin, ether spot ETFs, say funds
data.text: Hong Kong conditionally approved its first spot bitcoin and ether exchange traded funds (ETFs) on Monday, money managers said, paving the way for the city to become Asia's first to accept the cryptocu...

data.title: Hong Kong set to approve its first spot bitcoin ETFs in April, sources say
data.text: Spot bitcoin exchange-traded funds could be launched in Hong Kong this month with the first approvals likely to be announced next week, two people familiar with the matter said.

data.title: Amid crypto turmoil, Hong Kong debuts first crypto futures ETFs

data.title: Asian ETFs gain as U.S. stocks stumble

data.title: 3 Hong Kong ETFs for Q2 2021
data.text: 3 Hong Kong ETFs for Q2 2021 are FLHK, ZHOK, and EWH. All 3 trade in the U.S.

data.title: Hong Kong ETFs tumble as China moves to enact new security laws
data.text: Exchange-traded funds with exposure to Hong Kong sold off Friday after Beijing said it was considering new security laws that would curtail the territory's autonomy
